assertions.title: benchmark_node.sh performs toolchain verification and aborts on mismatch

steps.expected: The runner script parses cleanly before deeper validation.
steps.observed: Command exited 0 with no syntax errors.

steps.action: Run targeted pytest nodes `tests/infra/test_bench_infra_layout.py::TestBenchmarkNodeScript::test_verifies_toolchain_lock_and_ornn_bench_version` and `tests/infra/test_benchmark_node_runner.py::TestBenchmarkNodeRunnerExecution::test_aborts_on_toolchain_mismatch` with `--basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest`.
steps.expected: Static inspection should confirm the version/SHA checks exist, and the stub harness should fail before any runs start when the installed CLI version is wrong.
steps.observed: Both tests passed inside the 8-test targeted pytest run.

steps.expected: The mismatch case records a toolchain failure and leaves `COMPLETED_RUNS=0`.
steps.observed: `FAIL_REASON="ornn-bench version mismatch: expected 0.1.0, found 9.9.9."` and `COMPLETED_RUNS="0"`; no reports were produced.

terminal: /Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -vv --basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest <8 targeted infra tests> -> 8 passed in 1.49s
staticInspection: benchmark_node.sh grep hits show `verify_toolchain()` at line 443 calling `verify_python_pin`, `verify_ornn_bench_pin`, `verify_torch_pin`, `verify_cuda_pin`, `verify_driver_pin`, `verify_nvbandwidth_pin`, and `verify_nccl_pin` (lines 449-455).
benchmark_node.sh line 837 calls `verify_toolchain` before `enforce_sxm5_gate` or any benchmark runs.

assertions.title: benchmark_node.sh enforces SXM5 gate via topology + capacity

steps.expected: The script should require 8 GPUs, >=79000 MiB each, and NVLink (`NV*`) topology; a PCIe-only topology should abort and print topology data.
steps.observed: Both tests passed in the targeted pytest run.

steps.expected: The passing stub harness records 8 GPUs, 80GB-class memory, and NV-linked rows.
steps.observed: `ACTUAL_GPU_COUNT="8"`, each `GPU_*_MEMORY_MIB="81920"`, and `TOPOLOGY_ROWS_WITH_NV="8"`.

steps.expected: A bad topology should fail with an explanatory reason and the captured topology output.
steps.observed: `FAIL_REASON="Expected every GPU row to expose NV* connectivity; topology appears PCIe-only or incomplete."`; `topology.txt` shows `SYS` connectivity across the matrix.

terminal: /Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -vv --basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest <8 targeted infra tests> -> 8 passed in 1.49s
staticInspection: benchmark_node.sh defines `EXPECTED_GPU_COUNT=8` and `MEMORY_FLOOR_MIB=79000`, and grep confirms `enforce_sxm5_gate()` is present with topology handling.
The script uses `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total,driver_version --format=csv,noheader,nounits` plus `nvidia-smi topo -m` to enforce the gate.

assertions.title: benchmark_node.sh runs 3 benchmark runs with cooldown

steps.expected: The script should perform three sequential captures and preserve per-run artifacts.
steps.observed: Both tests passed in the targeted pytest run.

steps.expected: The manifest should record `RUN_COUNT=3`, `COMPLETED_RUNS=3`, and there should be three report files.
steps.observed: `RUN_COUNT="3"`, `COMPLETED_RUNS="3"`, and `reports/` contains `run-01.json`, `run-02.json`, and `run-03.json`.

steps.action: Inspect `/private/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest/test_completes_three_runs_reco0/ornn-bench-invocations.log` and static grep output from `benchmark_node.sh`.
steps.expected: Exactly three `ornn-bench run` invocations should be recorded and a cooldown `sleep` should be present between runs.
steps.observed: Invocation log contains three run entries; static inspection shows `run_single_capture 1/2/3` at lines 842-844 and `sleep "${COOLDOWN_SECONDS}"` at line 817.

terminal: /Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -vv --basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest <8 targeted infra tests> -> 8 passed in 1.49s

assertions.title: benchmark_node.sh uses explicit output paths and recovers default output if needed

steps.action: Run `tests/infra/test_benchmark_node_runner.py::TestBenchmarkNodeRunnerExecution::test_completes_three_runs_recovers_default_output_and_preserves_secret` within the targeted pytest selection.
steps.expected: The stub harness should force `ornn-bench` to emit `ornn_report_*.json`, and the runner should recover that default output into the explicit `-o` path.
steps.observed: The test passed.

steps.expected: The run manifest should record an explicit `-o` command and `RECOVERED_FROM_DEFAULT=1`.
steps.observed: `COMMAND` includes `run -o /private/tmp/.../reports/run-01.json --upload` and `RECOVERED_FROM_DEFAULT="1"`.

steps.expected: Recovered reports should exist at the requested explicit paths after fallback handling.
steps.observed: `run-01.json`, `run-02.json`, and `run-03.json` exist at the requested report paths.

terminal: /Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -vv --basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest <8 targeted infra tests> -> 8 passed in 1.49s
staticInspection: benchmark_node.sh line 755 builds `report_path` and executes `"${ORNN_BENCH_BIN}" run -o "${report_path}"`.
benchmark_node.sh line 658 defines `recover_default_report()` and line 790 calls it when the explicit path is missing.
benchmark_node.sh line 676 warns: `Recovered the default report into place.`

steps.expected: The script should gate upload via env, avoid persisting `ORNN_API_KEY`, and keep secrets out of campaign artifacts.
steps.observed: Both tests passed in the targeted pytest run.

steps.expected: Artifacts should record only upload state/presence bits, never the secret value itself.
steps.observed: Campaign manifest records `ORNN_API_KEY_PRESENT="1"` but no `ORNN_API_KEY` value, and the invocation log contains only `upload=1` flags.

steps.action: Rely on the stub test’s artifact scan across the entire success campaign directory.
steps.expected: The secret string should not appear in any generated artifact.
steps.observed: The passing test asserted that `secret-benchmark-key` was absent from all collected artifact text.

terminal: /Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -vv --basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest <8 targeted infra tests> -> 8 passed in 1.49s
staticInspection: benchmark_node.sh line 140 writes only `ORNN_API_KEY_PRESENT`, not the key itself.
Static layout test forbids `printf`, `echo`, redirection, or manifest writes containing `ORNN_API_KEY`.

assertions.title: benchmark_node.sh produces OrnnBench reports with local scores

steps.action: Run `tests/infra/test_benchmark_node_runner.py::TestBenchmarkNodeRunnerExecution::test_completes_three_runs_recovers_default_output_and_preserves_secret` as part of the targeted pytest selection.
steps.expected: A successful stub campaign should yield three scored JSON reports with populated manifests.
steps.observed: The test passed.

steps.expected: Three report files should exist and each should contain a populated `scores` object and non-empty `manifest`.
steps.observed: The reports directory contains `run-01.json`, `run-02.json`, and `run-03.json`; `run-01.json` includes `scores.ornn_i`, `scores.ornn_t`, `scores.status`, `scores.components`, and `manifest.artifacts=["stub"]`.

steps.action: Inspect `run-01.env` for the exact runner command.
steps.expected: The report should come from an `ornn-bench run -o <path>` invocation inside the campaign flow.
steps.observed: `COMMAND` records `/private/tmp/.../tools/bin/ornn-bench run -o /private/tmp/.../reports/run-01.json --upload`.

terminal: /Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -vv --basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest <8 targeted infra tests> -> 8 passed in 1.49s
staticInspection: benchmark_node.sh line 680 defines `verify_report_payload()` and requires `scores` plus `manifest` before marking a run complete.

steps.action: Inspect `/Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/ornn-bench-infra/toolchain.env` and run the targeted pytest nodes covering version verification and mismatch aborts.
steps.expected: The success path should record the pinned CLI version, and the mismatch path should abort with a version error.
steps.observed: `toolchain.env` pins `ORNN_BENCH_VERSION="0.1.0"`; both targeted tests passed.

steps.expected: The verification manifest should show equal expected/actual CLI versions.
steps.observed: `ORNN_BENCH_EXPECTED_VERSION="0.1.0"` and `ORNN_BENCH_ACTUAL_VERSION="0.1.0"`.

steps.expected: A mismatched CLI version should stop the campaign immediately.
steps.observed: `FAIL_REASON="ornn-bench version mismatch: expected 0.1.0, found 9.9.9."` and `COMPLETED_RUNS="0"`.

terminal: /Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -vv --basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest <8 targeted infra tests> -> 8 passed in 1.49s
staticInspection: benchmark_node.sh line 358 reads `ornn-bench --version` and compares it against `ORNN_BENCH_VERSION`.

assertions.title: Upload uses env-provided API key and can be toggled

steps.action: Run `tests/infra/test_benchmark_node_runner.py::TestBenchmarkNodeRunnerExecution::test_completes_three_runs_recovers_default_output_and_preserves_secret` with `upload_mode=auto` and an API key via the stub harness (inside the targeted pytest selection).
steps.expected: When `ORNN_API_KEY` is present, the runner should pass `--upload` on all three captures.
steps.observed: Success campaign invocation log contains three entries ending with `upload=1`, and `run-01.env` records the `--upload` flag.

steps.action: Run `tests/infra/test_benchmark_node_runner.py::TestBenchmarkNodeRunnerExecution::test_skips_upload_when_api_key_is_not_set` with `upload_mode=auto` and no API key (inside the targeted pytest selection).
steps.expected: Without `ORNN_API_KEY`, the runner should omit `--upload` on all three captures.
steps.observed: The no-key invocation log contains three entries ending with `upload=0`; `run-01.env` omits `--upload` and the campaign manifest records `UPLOAD_ENABLED="0"`.

steps.action: Inspect static upload gating logic in `benchmark_node.sh`.
steps.expected: Upload should be controlled only by `ORNN_BENCH_UPLOAD`/`ORNN_API_KEY`, with `always` mode failing when the key is absent.
steps.observed: Static grep shows `UPLOAD_SETTING` at line 15 plus auto/always/never resolution and the `ORNN_API_KEY` presence check at lines 262-270.

terminal: /Users/kushbavaria/Documents/ornn-benchmarking/.venv/bin/python -m pytest -vv --basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest <8 targeted infra tests> -> 8 passed in 1.49s
staticInspection: benchmark_node.sh lines 262-270 enable upload only when `ORNN_API_KEY` is present in `auto` mode and hard-fail `always` mode without a key.

frictions.description: On macOS, artifacts created under `--basetemp=/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest` resolved under `/private/tmp/...`, so artifact inspection had to follow the resolved path rather than the `/tmp` symlink literal.
frictions.resolution: Used the resolved `/private/tmp/...` manifest/report paths after pytest finished; all evidence stayed within the assigned namespace root.

summary: Tested 8 assigned assertions: 8 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. `bash -n` succeeded, targeted pytest ran the 8 relevant bench-infra runner tests in 1.49s, and stub campaign artifacts under `/private/tmp/benchinfra-runner-pytest` confirmed toolchain pin enforcement, SXM5 gating, 3-run cadence, fallback report recovery, scored report output, secret hygiene, and env-gated upload behavior.
